speaker: JAILER
text: You shall not now be stol’n; you have locks upon you.

speaker: JAILER
text: So graze as you find pasture.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              JAILER
text: Ay, or a stomach.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Most welcome, bondage, for thou art a way,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: I think, to liberty. Yet am I better

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Than one that’s sick o’ th’ gout, since he had rather

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Groan so in perpetuity than be cured

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: By th’ sure physician, Death, who is the key

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: T’ unbar these locks. My conscience, thou art fettered

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: More than my shanks and wrists. You good gods, give me

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: The penitent instrument to pick that bolt,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Then free forever. Is ’t enough I am sorry?

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: So children temporal fathers do appease;

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Gods are more full of mercy. Must I repent,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: I cannot do it better than in gyves,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Desired more than constrained. To satisfy,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: If of my freedom ’tis the main part, take

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: No stricter render of me than my all.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: I know you are more clement than vile men,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Who of their broken debtors take a third,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: A sixth, a tenth, letting them thrive again

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: On their abatement. That’s not my desire.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: For Imogen’s dear life take mine; and though

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: ’Tis not so dear, yet ’tis a life; you coined it.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: ’Tween man and man they weigh not every stamp;

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Though light, take pieces for the figure’s sake;

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: You rather mine, being yours. And so, great powers,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: If you will take this audit, take this life

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: And cancel these cold bonds. O Imogen,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: I’ll speak to thee in silence.

speaker: SICILIUS
text: No more, thou Thunder-master, show

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Thy spite on mortal flies.

speaker: SICILIUS
text: With Mars fall out, with Juno chide,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: That thy adulteries

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Rates and revenges.

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Hath my poor boy done aught but well,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Whose face I never saw?

speaker: SICILIUS
text: I died whilst in the womb he stayed,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Attending nature’s law;

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Whose father then—as men report

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Thou orphans’ father art—

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Thou shouldst have been, and shielded him

speaker: SICILIUS
text: From this earth-vexing smart.

speaker: MOTHER
text: Lucina lent not me her aid,

speaker: MOTHER
text: But took me in my throes,

speaker: MOTHER
text: That from me was Posthumus ripped,

speaker: MOTHER
text: Came crying ’mongst his foes,

speaker: MOTHER
text: A thing of pity.

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Great Nature, like his ancestry,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Molded the stuff so fair

speaker: SICILIUS
text: That he deserved the praise o’ th’ world

speaker: SICILIUS
text: As great Sicilius’ heir.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: When once he was mature for man,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: In Britain where was he

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: That could stand up his parallel

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: Or fruitful object be

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: In eye of Imogen, that best

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: Could deem his dignity?

speaker: MOTHER
text: With marriage wherefore was he mocked,

speaker: MOTHER
text: To be exiled and thrown

speaker: MOTHER
text: From Leonati seat, and cast

speaker: MOTHER
text: From her, his dearest one,

speaker: MOTHER
text: Sweet Imogen?

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Why did you suffer Iachimo,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Slight thing of Italy,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: To taint his nobler heart and brain

speaker: SICILIUS
text: With needless jealousy,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: And to become the geck and scorn

speaker: SICILIUS
text: O’ th’ other’s villainy?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: For this, from stiller seats we came,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: Our parents and us twain,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: That striking in our country’s cause

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: Fell bravely and were slain,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: Our fealty and Tenantius’ right

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: With honor to maintain.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: Like hardiment Posthumus hath

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: To Cymbeline performed.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: Then, Jupiter, thou king of gods,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: Why hast thou thus adjourned

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: The graces for his merits due,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BROTHER
text: Being all to dolors turned?

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Thy crystal window ope; look out.

speaker: SICILIUS
text: No longer exercise

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Upon a valiant race thy harsh

speaker: SICILIUS
text: And potent injuries.

speaker: MOTHER
text: Since, Jupiter, our son is good,

speaker: MOTHER
text: Take off his miseries.

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Peep through thy marble mansion. Help,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Or we poor ghosts will cry

speaker: SICILIUS
text: To th’ shining synod of the rest

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Against thy deity.

speaker: BROTHERS
text: Help, Jupiter, or we appeal

speaker: BROTHERS
text: And from thy justice fly.

speaker: JUPITER
text: No more, you petty spirits of region low,

speaker: JUPITER
text: Offend our hearing! Hush. How dare you ghosts

speaker: JUPITER
text: Accuse the Thunderer, whose bolt, you know,

speaker: JUPITER
text: Sky-planted, batters all rebelling coasts.

speaker: JUPITER
text: Poor shadows of Elysium, hence, and rest

speaker: JUPITER
text: Upon your never-withering banks of flowers.

speaker: JUPITER
text: Be not with mortal accidents oppressed.

speaker: JUPITER
text: No care of yours it is; you know ’tis ours.

speaker: JUPITER
text: Whom best I love I cross, to make my gift,

speaker: JUPITER
text: The more delayed, delighted. Be content.

speaker: JUPITER
text: Your low-laid son our godhead will uplift.

speaker: JUPITER
text: His comforts thrive, his trials well are spent.

speaker: JUPITER
text: Our Jovial star reigned at his birth, and in

speaker: JUPITER
text: Our temple was he married. Rise, and fade.

speaker: JUPITER
text: He shall be lord of Lady Imogen,

speaker: JUPITER
text: And happier much by his affliction made.

speaker: JUPITER
text: This tablet lay upon his breast, wherein

speaker: JUPITER
text: Our pleasure his full fortune doth confine.

speaker: JUPITER
text: And so away. No farther with your din

speaker: JUPITER
text: Express impatience, lest you stir up mine.—

speaker: JUPITER
text: Mount, eagle, to my palace crystalline.

speaker: SICILIUS
text: He came in thunder. His celestial breath

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Was sulphurous to smell. The holy eagle

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Stooped as to foot us. His ascension is

speaker: SICILIUS
text: More sweet than our blest fields; his royal bird

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Preens the immortal wing and cloys his beak,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: As when his god is pleased.

speaker: ALL
text: Thanks, Jupiter.

speaker: SICILIUS
text: The marble pavement closes; he is entered

speaker: SICILIUS
text: His radiant roof. Away, and, to be blest,

speaker: SICILIUS
text: Let us with care perform his great behest.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Sleep, thou hast been a grandsire and begot

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: A father to me, and thou hast created

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: A mother and two brothers. But, O scorn,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Gone! They went hence so soon as they were born.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: And so I am awake. Poor wretches that depend

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: On greatness’ favor dream as I have done,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Wake, and find nothing. But, alas, I swerve.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Many dream not to find, neither deserve,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: And yet are steeped in favors; so am I

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: That have this golden chance and know not why.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: What fairies haunt this ground? A book? O rare one,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Be not, as is our fangled world, a garment

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Nobler than that it covers. Let thy effects

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: So follow, to be, most unlike our courtiers,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: As good as promise.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: ’Tis still a dream, or else such stuff as madmen

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Tongue and brain not; either both or nothing,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Or senseless speaking, or a speaking such

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: As sense cannot untie. Be what it is,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: The action of my life is like it, which

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: I’ll keep, if but for sympathy.

speaker: JAILER
text: Come, sir, are you ready for death?

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Over-roasted rather; ready long ago.

speaker: JAILER
text: Hanging is the word, sir. If you be ready for

speaker: JAILER
text: that, you are well cooked.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: So, if I prove a good repast to the spectators,

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: the dish pays the shot.

speaker: JAILER
text: A heavy reckoning for you, sir. But the comfort

speaker: JAILER
text: is, you shall be called to no more payments, fear

speaker: JAILER
text: no more tavern bills, which are often the sadness

speaker: JAILER
text: of parting as the procuring of mirth. You come in

speaker: JAILER
text: faint for want of meat, depart reeling with too

speaker: JAILER
text: much drink; sorry that you have paid too much,

speaker: JAILER
text: and sorry that you are paid too much; purse and

speaker: JAILER
text: brain both empty; the brain the heavier for being

speaker: JAILER
text: too light; the purse too light, being drawn of heaviness.

speaker: JAILER
text: O, of this contradiction you shall now be

speaker: JAILER
text: quit. O, the charity of a penny cord! It sums up

speaker: JAILER
text: thousands in a trice. You have no true debitor and

speaker: JAILER
text: creditor but it; of what’s past, is, and to come, the

speaker: JAILER
text: discharge. Your neck, sir, is pen, book, and counters;

speaker: JAILER
text: so the acquittance follows.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: I am merrier to die than thou art to live.

speaker: JAILER
text: Indeed, sir, he that sleeps feels not the

speaker: JAILER
text: toothache. But a man that were to sleep your

speaker: JAILER
text: sleep, and a hangman to help him to bed, I think

speaker: JAILER
text: he would change places with his officer; for, look

speaker: JAILER
text: you, sir, you know not which way you shall go.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Yes, indeed do I, fellow.

speaker: JAILER
text: Your Death has eyes in ’s head, then. I have not

speaker: JAILER
text: seen him so pictured. You must either be directed

speaker: JAILER
text: by some that take upon them to know, or to take

speaker: JAILER
text: upon yourself that which I am sure you do not

speaker: JAILER
text: know, or jump the after-inquiry on your own peril.

speaker: JAILER
text: And how you shall speed in your journey’s end, I

speaker: JAILER
text: think you’ll never return to tell one.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: I tell thee, fellow, there are none want

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: eyes to direct them the way I am going but such as

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: wink and will not use them.

speaker: JAILER
text: What an infinite mock is this, that a man

speaker: JAILER
text: should have the best use of eyes to see the way of

speaker: JAILER
text: blindness! I am sure hanging’s the way of winking.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Knock off his manacles; bring your prisoner

speaker: MESSENGER
text: to the King.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Thou bring’st good news. I am called to be

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: made free.

speaker: JAILER
text: I’ll be hanged then.

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: Thou shalt be then freer than a jailer. No

speaker: POSTHUMUS
text: bolts for the dead.

speaker: JAILER
text: Unless a man would marry a gallows and beget

speaker: JAILER
text: young gibbets, I never saw one so prone. Yet, on my

speaker: JAILER
text: conscience, there are verier knaves desire to live,

speaker: JAILER
text: for all he be a Roman; and there be some of them

speaker: JAILER
text: too that die against their wills. So should I, if I

speaker: JAILER
text: were one. I would we were all of one mind, and

speaker: JAILER
text: one mind good. O, there were desolation of jailers

speaker: JAILER
text: and gallowses! I speak against my present profit,

speaker: JAILER
text: but my wish hath a preferment in ’t.
